title: 10 best herbal tinctures for stiff neck
intro_desc: Herbal tinctures for stiff neck are concentrated liquid extracts of healing herbs, which are used to alleviate tension, reduce inflammation, and improve mobility in the neck. These remedies often include a combination of potent herbs such as valerian root, skullcap, cramp bark, and Jamaican dogwood, which work together to relax muscles, soothe nerves, and minimize pain. By promoting circulation and reducing stiffness, herbal tinctures can significantly improve an individual's quality of life, enabling them to engage in daily activities with increased comfort and mobility. For instance, a person who suffered from limited neck mobility due to stiffness may find it easier to turn their head while driving, work at a computer, or play sports, enhancing their overall well-being and productivity.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Bell pepper tincture helps with stiff neck because it contains anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties that provide relief to sore and tense muscles. The capsaicin compound found in bell peppers increases blood flow and reduces inflammation, which can alleviate tension in the neck and promote healing. Additionally, bell pepper tincture can help relax muscles, ease pain, and improve mobility, making it an effective natural remedy for a stiff neck. Regular use of bell pepper tincture can help prevent stiff necks by keeping the muscles flexible and reducing inflammation.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Bell pepper tincture's anti-inflammatory properties, due to the presence of vitamin C and antioxidants like flavonoids, help reduce inflammation in the neck muscles, alleviating stiffness.
Analgesic: The analgesic effects of bell pepper tincture, attributed to capsaicin, aid in relieving pain associated with stiff neck by desensitizing pain receptors.
Muscle relaxant: The muscle relaxant properties of bell pepper tincture, resulting from the presence of potassium and magnesium, help soothe stiff neck muscles and promote relaxation.

Circulation improvement: Bell pepper tincture enhances blood circulation, owing to its high vitamin B6 content, which helps increase the supply of oxygen and nutrients to neck muscles, reducing stiffness.
Antispasmodic: The antispasmodic properties of bell pepper tincture, attributed to the presence of vitamin E and magnesium, aid in alleviating muscle spasms and reducing stiffness in the neck.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Contain high levels of antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds, which can help reduce stiffness and inflammation in the neck.
Stems: Rich in essential oils and minerals, stems can help improve blood circulation and alleviate muscle tension in the neck.
Flowers: Contain flavonoids and carotenoids, which have anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties, making them effective in reducing neck stiffness and pain.
Seeds: High in fatty acids and vitamins, seeds can help nourish and moisturize the neck muscles, reducing stiffness and promoting flexibility.
remedy_recipe: Chop two bell peppers and one cup of anti-inflammatory herbs such as turmeric and ginger, then combine in a clean jar.
Pour enough vodka or other high-proof alcohol into the jar to cover the plant material, ensuring a 1:2 herb-to-alcohol ratio.
Securely fasten a lid on the jar and store it in a cool, dark place for two to six weeks, shaking it daily.
After the designated time, strain the mixture through cheesecloth into a dark glass bottle, squeezing out all liquid.
Label the bottle with the ingredients, date, and recommended dosage of 30 drops up to three times daily for neck stiffness, then store in a cool, dark place.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Indian frankincense tincture helps with stiff neck because it has anti-inflammatory properties that can reduce inflammation and swelling in the neck muscles, thus alleviating stiffness. This herbal remedy contains boswellic acids, which have been shown to inhibit the production of inflammatory chemicals in the body. Additionally, frankincense has analgesic properties that can help relieve pain and discomfort associated with a stiff neck. Its soothing effects can help improve mobility and flexibility in the neck, allowing for easier movement and reduced tension. Overall, Indian frankincense tincture is a natural and effective way to find relief from a stiff neck.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Indian frankincense tincture's anti-inflammatory properties, due to boswellic acids, help reduce inflammation in the neck muscles and joints, alleviating stiffness.
Analgesic: The pain-relieving effects of Indian frankincense tincture, attributed to alpha-pinene and other compounds, help manage the pain associated with a stiff neck.
Muscle relaxant: The muscle relaxant properties of Indian frankincense tincture, thanks to incensole acetate, aid in reducing tension and stiffness in the neck muscles.
Improves blood flow: Indian frankincense tincture enhances blood circulation, allowing better nutrient and oxygen supply to neck tissues, promoting healing and reducing stiffness.
Antioxidant: The antioxidant properties of Indian frankincense tincture, from compounds like ascorbic acid, help protect neck tissues from oxidative stress and support overall recovery.
remedy_parts: Stems: The stems of the Indian frankincense tincture plant are used to make medicinal tea for stiff neck due to their anti-inflammatory properties.
Bark: The bark of the plant is used for its pain-relieving qualities, making it an ideal addition to a stiff neck tea.
Resin: The resin, or sap, of the Indian frankincense tincture plant is used for its soothing and calming effects on stiff muscles.
Buds: The buds of the plant are rich in essential oils and are used for their ability to improve circulation and reduce inflammation.
Leaves: The leaves of the Indian frankincense tincture plant are used for their antispasmodic properties, making them helpful in reducing muscle tension and stiffness.
remedy_recipe: Chop 1 cup of Indian frankincense resin into small pieces and place in a clean glass jar.
Add 2 cups of vodka or rum (80 proof) to the jar, ensuring the resin is fully submerged.
Seal the jar and store in a cool, dark place for 2-3 weeks, shaking daily.
Strain the mixture using cheesecloth, squeezing out the liquid and discarding the solid residue.
Store the resulting tincture in a tightly sealed glass bottle and use 1-2 mL up to three times daily for a stiff neck.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Turmeric tincture helps with stiff neck because it contains a powerful anti-inflammatory compound called curcumin, which reduces inflammation and pain in the affected muscles and joints. This natural remedy increases blood flow and relaxes the muscles, providing relief from stiffness and tension. Its potent antioxidant properties also help to repair damaged tissues and promote faster healing. Regular use of turmeric tincture can improve mobility, reduce stiffness, and alleviate discomfort in the neck, making it an effective and safe alternative to over-the-counter pain relievers.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Turmeric tincture's anti-inflammatory properties, due to the presence of curcumin, help reduce inflammation and swelling in the neck muscles, providing relief from stiffness.
Analgesic: The analgesic effects of turmeric tincture, attributed to curcumin, aid in alleviating pain associated with a stiff neck, offering comfort and promoting mobility.

Muscle relaxant: Turmeric tincture contains active constituents, such as curcumin and turmerones, that exhibit muscle relaxant properties, helping to loosen tight neck muscles and improve range of motion.

Antioxidant: The antioxidant properties of turmeric tincture, primarily from curcumin, help protect neck muscles from oxidative stress and damage, supporting overall muscle health and reducing stiffness.

Improved circulation: Turmeric tincture's ability to improve blood flow and circulation, thanks to its active compounds, ensures that nutrients and oxygen reach neck muscles, promoting healing and reducing stiffness.
remedy_parts: Rhizomes: Turmeric's rhizomes are rich in curcumin, which has anti-inflammatory properties that can help alleviate stiff neck.
Leaves: Turmeric leaves have a soothing effect, making them useful in reducing neck stiffness and promoting mobility.
Flowers: Turmeric flowers contain antioxidants that help reduce inflammation, thereby providing relief from a stiff neck.
Seeds: Turmeric seeds are known for their warming properties, which can help increase blood flow and reduce stiffness in the neck.
remedy_recipe: Chop 1 cup of fresh turmeric root into small pieces and place in a clean glass jar.
Add 2 cups of vodka or rum (80 proof) to the jar, ensuring the turmeric is completely submerged.
Seal the jar and store in a cool, dark place for 4-6 weeks, shaking it gently every few days.
After 4-6 weeks, strain the liquid through a cheesecloth into a clean glass bottle, squeezing out as much liquid as possible.
Label the bottle with the ingredients and date, and store in a cool, dark place for up to a year. Take 1-2 mL (1/2-1 teaspoon) up to three times a day for a stiff neck.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Ginger tincture helps with stiff neck because it contains anti-inflammatory compounds, such as gingerols and shogaols, which can reduce inflammation and alleviate pain in the affected muscles and joints. Ginger has been shown to inhibit the production of prostaglandins, hormone-like substances that cause pain and inflammation in the body. Additionally, ginger tincture can improve circulation and increase blood flow to the neck, providing oxygen and nutrients to the stiff muscles and promoting healing. The soothing properties of ginger tincture can help relax the tight muscles, reduce tension, and relieve stiffness, making it an effective natural remedy for neck pain and discomfort.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Ginger tincture's anti-inflammatory properties, due to compounds like gingerols and shogaols, help reduce inflammation in the neck muscles, providing relief from stiffness.
Pain-relieving: The pain-relieving effects of ginger tincture, attributed to its active constituents such as gingerols and zingibain, help alleviate discomfort associated with a stiff neck.
Muscle relaxation: Ginger tincture promotes muscle relaxation, thanks to its ability to inhibit prostaglandin and leukotriene synthesis, reducing tension in the neck muscles.
Improved blood circulation: Ginger tincture enhances blood flow and circulation, allowing for better nutrient and oxygen delivery to the neck muscles, promoting healing and reducing stiffness.

Antioxidant properties: The strong antioxidant properties of ginger tincture, derived from compounds like gingerols and shogaols, help protect neck muscles from oxidative stress and damage, supporting overall muscle health and reducing stiffness.
remedy_parts: Rhizomes: Ginger rhizomes are widely used to make medicinal tea for stiff neck due to their anti-inflammatory properties that help alleviate muscle tension.
Leaves: Ginger leaves are used in teas for their rich antioxidant content, which helps reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the neck muscles.
Flowers: Ginger flowers are occasionally used in teas for their mild analgesic properties, providing relief from neck pain and discomfort.
Seeds: Ginger seeds are a lesser-known addition to medicinal teas, as they contain compounds that help improve circulation, reducing stiffness in the neck.
remedy_recipe: Chop 1 cup of fresh ginger root into small pieces and place in a clean glass jar.
Pour 2 cups of vodka or rum over the ginger, ensuring it is fully submerged, and tightly seal the jar.
Store the jar in a cool, dark place for 2-4 weeks, shaking it gently every few days.
Strain the liquid through cheesecloth into a dark glass bottle, squeezing out any excess liquid from the ginger.
Label the bottle with the ingredients and date, and store in a cool, dark place for up to a year. Take 1-2 mL (1/2-1 teaspoon) up to three times a day as needed for a stiff neck.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Rosemary tincture helps with stiff neck because it contains anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties that can alleviate pain and reduce inflammation in the affected muscles and tissues. The active compounds in rosemary, such as rosmarinic acid and carnosic acid, work together to improve blood circulation, relax muscles, and provide a soothing effect on the neck area. By promoting relaxation and reducing tension, rosemary tincture can help ease stiffness and discomfort, allowing for greater mobility and improved range of motion in the neck. Its natural healing properties make it a popular alternative remedy for individuals seeking relief from neck stiffness and related symptoms.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Rosemary tincture's anti-inflammatory properties, due to its active constituents like carnosol and carnosic acid, can help reduce swelling and inflammation in the neck muscles, providing relief from stiffness.

Analgesic: The analgesic effects of rosemary tincture, attributed to its rosmarinic acid and essential oils, can alleviate pain and discomfort associated with a stiff neck, offering comfort and promoting mobility.

Muscle relaxant: Rosemary tincture's muscle relaxant properties, resulting from its rosmarinic acid and essential oils, can help loosen tight neck muscles, reducing stiffness and improving range of motion.

Circulation-enhancing: The circulation-enhancing properties of rosemary tincture, due to its camphene and α-pinene content, can increase blood flow to the neck area, promoting healing and reducing stiffness.

Antioxidant: The antioxidant properties of rosemary tincture, attributed to its rosmarinic acid and carnosol, can help protect neck muscles from oxidative stress and damage, supporting overall muscle health and reducing stiffness.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves of rosemary are rich in essential oils, which can help to relax muscles and reduce inflammation, making them ideal for relieving stiff neck.
Stems: The stems of rosemary contain rosmarinic acid, which has been shown to have anti-inflammatory properties, making them useful for reducing stiffness and discomfort in the neck.
Flowers: The flowers of rosemary contain antioxidants that can help to reduce inflammation and promote healing, making them a valuable addition to a medicinal tea for stiff neck.
Seeds: The seeds of rosemary are rich in nutrients and antioxidants, which can help to support overall health and well-being, and may contribute to reduced stiffness and discomfort in the neck.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh rosemary leaves, filling a clean, dry mason jar about three-quarters full.
Pour 100 proof vodka over the leaves until they are completely submerged, then secure the lid.
Store the jar in a cool, dark place for four to six weeks, shaking it gently every few days.
After the allotted time, strain the mixture using cheesecloth, squeezing out the excess liquid and discarding the plant material.
Transfer the resulting tincture to a sterilized glass dropper bottle for easy use, storing it in a cool, dark location. Take 1-2 dropperfuls up to three times a day for a stiff neck.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: White willow tincture helps with stiff neck because it contains salicin, a compound that has anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties. These properties help to reduce inflammation and alleviate pain in the neck muscles, making it easier to move and relieving discomfort. Additionally, white willow tincture has been used for centuries as a natural remedy for various types of pain and inflammation, making it a popular choice for those looking for a more holistic approach to managing stiff neck. Its effectiveness is often attributed to its ability to inhibit the production of prostaglandins, which are hormone-like substances that cause pain and inflammation in the body.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: White willow tincture contains salicin, which is a natural anti-inflammatory agent that helps reduce inflammation and swelling in the neck muscles, providing relief from stiffness.
Analgesic: The salicin in white willow tincture is converted to salicylic acid in the body, acting as a painkiller that alleviates the pain associated with a stiff neck.
Fever reducer: White willow tincture can help reduce fever, which can sometimes accompany a stiff neck, as a result of its salicin content.
Muscle relaxant: White willow tincture contains tannins that have astringent properties, helping to relax and soothe tight neck muscles, thereby reducing stiffness.

Antioxidant: White willow tincture has antioxidant properties due to its flavonoids content, which helps protect the neck muscles from damage caused by free radicals, promoting overall muscle health and reducing stiffness.
remedy_parts: Bark: The bark of the white willow tree is commonly used to make medicinal tea for stiff neck due to its high salicin content, which has anti-inflammatory and pain-relieving properties.
Leaves: The leaves of the white willow plant are used in teas for stiff neck because they contain salicin, a compound that can help reduce inflammation and alleviate pain.
Rhyzomes: White willow rhyzomes are used in medicinal teas for stiff neck due to their ability to provide pain relief and reduce inflammation.
Stems: The stems of the white willow plant contain salicin, a compound that can help alleviate pain and reduce inflammation, making them useful for medicinal teas for stiff neck.
remedy_recipe: Chop 1 cup of white willow bark into small pieces and place in a clean jar.
Pour 2 cups of 100-proof vodka or pure grain alcohol over the bark, ensuring it is fully submerged.
Seal the jar and store in a cool, dark place for 2-4 weeks, shaking daily.
Strain the mixture using cheesecloth, squeezing out the excess liquid.
Store the tincture in a clean, dark glass bottle and use 1-2 mL up to three times daily for a stiff neck.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: English lavender tincture helps with stiff neck because it contains anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties that can alleviate muscle tension and discomfort. The active compounds in lavender, such as linalool and linalyl acetate, work to reduce inflammation and ease muscle spasms, thereby promoting relaxation and alleviating stiffness in the neck. Additionally, lavender has been shown to improve circulation, which can further aid in reducing tension and promoting healing in the affected area. By using a lavender tincture, individuals can experience natural relief from stiff neck and improve their overall well-being.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: English lavender tincture's anti-inflammatory property helps reduce inflammation in the neck muscles, thereby alleviating stiffness. This property is due to the presence of compounds like linalyl acetate and linalool.

Analgesic: Its analgesic effect helps manage pain associated with a stiff neck by inhibiting pain perception in the brain. The active constituents responsible for this property include caryophyllene and linalool.

Muscle relaxant: English lavender tincture has a muscle-relaxing effect, which helps loosen tight neck muscles and reduce stiffness. The active constituents responsible for this property include linalool and lavandulyl acetate.

Sedative: The sedative property of English lavender tincture promotes relaxation and reduces tension in the neck muscles, further alleviating stiffness. This property is attributed to the presence of linalool and terpinen-4-ol.

Circulatory stimulant: English lavender tincture improves blood flow to the neck area, promoting healing and reducing stiffness. The active constituents responsible for this property include 1,8-cineole and α-pinene.
remedy_parts: Flowers: Rich in calming agents, English lavender flowers are used in medicinal tea for stiff neck due to their soothing properties.
Leaves: Containing high levels of flavonoids, English lavender leaves are beneficial in medicinal tea for stiff neck as they have anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic effects.
Buds: Packed with essential oils, English lavender buds are excellent for medicinal tea for stiff neck because they help alleviate tension and promote relaxation.
remedy_recipe: Harvest English lavender flowers, filling a clean, dry mason jar about 3/4 full.
Add 80-proof vodka to cover the flowers, leaving a 1-inch headspace.
Securely seal the jar and store it in a cool, dark place for 4-6 weeks, shaking it gently once a day.
After the maceration period, strain the mixture using cheesecloth into a dark glass bottle, squeezing out the liquid from the flowers.
Label the bottle with the contents, date, and dosage (typically 20-30 drops up to three times a day for a stiff neck) and store it in a cool, dark place.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: St. John's wort tincture helps with stiff neck because it contains anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties that can alleviate muscle tension and reduce pain. The herb's active compounds, such as hypericin and hyperforin, work together to promote blood flow and relax muscles, thereby easing stiffness and discomfort in the neck. Additionally, St. John's wort tincture has been shown to have a calming effect on the nervous system, which can help to reduce stress and tension that can contribute to a stiff neck. Its soothing properties make it an ideal natural remedy for relieving stiffness and promoting mobility in the neck.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: St. John's wort tincture's anti-inflammatory properties, due to the presence of hypericin and hyperforin, help reduce swelling and inflammation in the neck muscles, providing relief from stiffness.
Analgesic: The analgesic effects of St. John's wort, attributed to hypericin and other components, help alleviate pain associated with a stiff neck, offering comfort and promoting mobility.

Muscle relaxant: St. John's wort tincture contains muscle-relaxing compounds, such as hypericin and hyperforin, that help release tension in the neck muscles, reducing stiffness and improving range of motion.

Antispasmodic: The antispasmodic properties of St. John's wort, resulting from active constituents like hypericin, aid in preventing and easing muscle spasms, which can contribute to a stiff neck and discomfort.

Improved blood flow: St. John's wort tincture promotes better blood circulation due to its active components, including hypericin and hyperforin, which help increase the supply of oxygen and nutrients to the neck muscles, aiding in healing and reducing stiffness.
remedy_parts: Flowers: The flowers of St. John's Wort are commonly used in making medicinal tea for stiff neck due to their anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties.
Leaves: The leaves of St. John's Wort contain high levels of hypericin and hyperforin, which are believed to help relieve muscle tension and stiffness.

Stems: Although less potent than the flowers and leaves, the stems of St. John's Wort can still contribute to the tea's therapeutic effects, providing additional anti-inflammatory and relaxing properties.
remedy_recipe: Harvest St. John's Wort flowers and leaves, filling a clean, dry jar about 3/4 full.
Submerge the herbs completely with a menstruum of 50% vodka and 50% distilled water, leaving a 1-inch headspace.
Seal the jar and store it in a cool, dark place for 4-6 weeks, shaking it gently every few days.
Strain the tincture through cheesecloth into a clean, dark glass bottle, squeezing out the marc.
Label the bottle with the contents, date, and dosage (typically 30-60 drops up to three times a day for a stiff neck), and store it in a cool, dark place.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Arnica flower tincture helps with stiff neck because it contains anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce swelling and pain in the affected area. Arnica has been shown to increase blood flow and improve the range of motion in joints, making it an effective treatment for stiff necks. Additionally, arnica's analgesic properties can help alleviate muscle tension and discomfort, providing relief to individuals suffering from stiff necks. Its natural properties make it a safe and gentle alternative to over-the-counter pain medications.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Arnica flower tincture's anti-inflammatory properties, due to its active constituents like sesquiterpene lactones and helenalin, help reduce inflammation in the neck muscles, thereby alleviating stiffness.
Analgesic: Arnica flower tincture contains analgesic compounds, such as flavonoids and volatile oils, which help in relieving pain caused by a stiff neck.

Circulatory stimulant: Arnica flower tincture promotes blood circulation, thanks to its active constituents like thymol and carvacrol, which aids in healing stiff neck by increasing the supply of oxygen and nutrients to the affected area.
Muscle relaxant: Arnica flower tincture has muscle relaxant properties, attributed to its active constituents like tannins and phenolic acids, which help in reducing muscle tension and stiffness.

Antispasmodic: Arnica flower tincture's antispasmodic properties, due to its active constituents like essential oils and resin, help in alleviating muscle spasms and cramps, thereby providing relief from a stiff neck.
remedy_parts: Flowers: The arnica flower contains numerous sesquiterpene lactones, which are thought to have anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties, making it ideal for treating stiff neck.
Leaves: Arnica leaves also contain these beneficial compounds, and when used in a medicinal tea, they can help reduce inflammation and alleviate pain in the neck.
Rhyzomes: Arnica rhyzomes have similar properties to the flowers and leaves, and when used in a tea, they can help improve circulation, reduce swelling, and soothe sore muscles in the neck.
remedy_recipe: Gather 2-3 handfuls of fresh arnica flowers and let them wilt for 12 hours to reduce moisture content.
Fill a clean jar with the wilted arnica flowers, leaving 1-2 inches of space at the top.
Pour 100-proof vodka over the flowers until they are completely submerged, ensuring a 1:2 ratio of herb to menstruum.
Seal the jar and store it in a cool, dark place for 4-6 weeks, shaking it gently every few days.
Strain the tincture using cheesecloth, squeezing out the liquid, and store it in a dark glass bottle away from direct sunlight.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Feverfew tincture helps with stiff neck because it contains parthenolide, a compound that has anti-inflammatory and pain-relieving properties. Stiff neck is often caused by muscle tension and inflammation, and feverfew tincture can help alleviate these symptoms. The herb works by inhibiting the production of inflammatory chemicals in the body, which can reduce swelling and discomfort in the neck muscles. Additionally, feverfew has been shown to improve blood flow and relax muscle spasms, further helping to relieve stiffness and promote mobility. By addressing the underlying inflammation and tension, feverfew tincture can provide effective relief for stiff neck and promote overall musculoskeletal health.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Feverfew tincture's anti-inflammatory properties, due to parthenolide and other sesquiterpene lactones, help reduce inflammation and swelling in stiff neck muscles.
Analgesic: The analgesic effects of feverfew, attributed to parthenolide and other active compounds, help alleviate pain associated with stiff necks.
Muscle relaxant: Feverfew contains compounds such as parthenolide and tanetin, which exhibit muscle relaxant properties, aiding in reducing muscle tension and spasms in the neck.
Antispasmodic: Feverfew's antispasmodic properties, from compounds like parthenolide, help relieve muscle spasms and cramping, providing comfort for stiff necks.

Circulation improvement: Feverfew may improve blood flow and reduce blood vessel constriction, allowing for better nutrient and oxygen delivery to affected neck muscles, promoting healing and reducing stiffness.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves of feverfew are commonly used in teas for treating stiff neck due to their anti-inflammatory properties, which can help alleviate muscle tension and pain.
Flowers: Feverfew flowers contain parthenolide, a compound known for its ability to reduce inflammation and muscle spasms, making them useful in medicinal teas for stiff neck.
Seeds: Feverfew seeds are rich in essential oils and parthenolide, which can help relax muscles and reduce inflammation, making them beneficial for medicinal tea to treat stiff neck.
remedy_recipe: Chop fresh feverfew leaves and flowers (Tanacetum parthenium) to fill a clean, dry mason jar, leaving 1 inch of space at the top.
Pour 80-proof vodka over the herbs until they are completely submerged, covering with a non-reactive lid.
Store the jar in a cool, dark place for 4-6 weeks, shaking it gently every few days.
Strain the tincture using cheesecloth or a fine-mesh strainer, squeezing out the liquid and discarding the spent herbs.

Store the finished feverfew tincture in a tightly sealed glass bottle, away from direct sunlight, for up to a year. For a stiff neck, take 1-2 mL (20-40 drops) of the tincture 3 times a day, as needed.

supplementary_best_treatment: The best way to treat stiff neck with herbal tinctures is by using anti-inflammatory and analgesic herbs such as turmeric, willow bark, and Jamaican dogwood. These herbs, when taken internally, can help reduce inflammation, relieve pain, and promote blood flow, which can greatly alleviate stiff neck symptoms. It is important to follow the recommended dosage and consult with a healthcare provider before starting any new treatment. Additionally, applying a warm compress and gently massaging the affected area can enhance the tincture's effectiveness.
supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to stiff neck that are treated with herbal tinctures include muscle tension, inflammation, and pain. Herbs like turmeric and willow bark are known for their anti-inflammatory properties, which can help alleviate symptoms of a stiff neck. Valerian root and passionflower are often used as natural relaxants to reduce muscle tension. Additionally, ginger and cayenne pepper can improve circulation and provide pain relief. It is important to consult with a healthcare professional before starting any new herbal treatment.
